revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}.
[[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[ja:加努弥神社]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}.

No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}}

No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}.

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3

This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}.

The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.

In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|Kanumi Shrine]] | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|17.8|N|136|44|51.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).

Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. '''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.

In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.

They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.

'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft|12=draft}} used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} operated by JR Central. * From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 加努弥神社 |画像= [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|加努弥神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]鹿海町字大野間1100-2<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php 『式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館｜125社5』]（式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館、2015年5月3日閲覧）</ref> |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 17.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 51.6 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 稲依比女命 |社格= [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''加努弥神社'''（かぬみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|末社]]。内宮の末社16社のうち、第9位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]鹿海町（かのみちょう、かのめちょう）、田んぼの中に鎮座する<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：108ページ</ref>。鹿海の[[集落]]からは少し離れている<ref name="ic"/>。かつては[[五十鈴川]]が社地のすぐ近くを流れており、船着場もあったとされる<ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：60ページ</ref>。
[[神体]]は[[石]]で、[[石畳]]の上に祀られている<ref name="ic"/>。[[社殿]]はない<ref name="gk"/>。
[[祭神]]は稲依比女命（いなよりひめのみこと）<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。[[年神|大歳神]]の子で、五穀の[[守護神]]である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。
社地の[[面積]]は1畝7歩（≒122[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）ある<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ</ref>。神宮125社のうち、内宮・[[豊受大神宮|外宮]]両宮域外に鎮座する神社の中で最も社地の狭い神社である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=コース08 朝熊道ルート 〜朝熊山麓と五十鈴川沿いの神社を訪ねる〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 創建不詳。伊勢神宮末社の定義より『皇大神宮儀式帳』成立、すなわち[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在した<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ</ref>ことになる。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]の創設により、内宮末社でありながら、[[度会県]]管轄の村社に列し、伊勢神宮と度会県から二重に所管されることとなった<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：271ページ</ref>。こうした二重管轄はほかの神宮摂末社でも発生したため、神宮司庁は[[教部省]]に諮ったところ、11の末社が神宮の管轄から外れた一方、加努弥神社と外宮摂社の[[御食神社 (伊勢市)|御食神社]]はそのまま二重管轄とすることが[[明治5年]]4月（グレゴリオ暦：1872年5月）に申し渡された<ref name="st1"/>。
加努弥神社では[[1881年]]（明治14年）12月に[[氏子]]が内宮末社と村社の分離を求めた<ref name="st6">櫻井（1991）：276ページ</ref>。鹿海の住民は、明治4年に本来は鎮座地も祭神も異なっていた内宮末社と村社が混同され、事実上神宮所管となり、村社として住民が管理できなくなってしまったと訴えた<ref name="st6"/>。更に、[[明治3年]]（[[1870年]]）に村社「八王子社」が「加努弥神社」に改称され、祭神も加努弥神社の祭神に改められたという書類と「加努弥神社」の旧社地が「八王子社」の社地と異なることを示す書類を証拠として提出した<ref name="st7">櫻井（1991）：277ページ</ref>。神宮・[[三重県庁]]・[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]の三者で協議を重ねた結果、[[1886年]]（明治19年）2月に内宮末社と村社を分離することで決着した<ref name="st7"/>。同じく二重管轄とされた御食神社には住民の分離運動が行われたという記録はなく、分離問題はなかったと見られている<ref>櫻井（1991）：278ページ</ref>。
== 交通 == 社地は[[農道]]に面している<ref name="ic"/>。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[五十鈴ケ丘駅]]より徒歩約16分（約1.3km）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[五十鈴川駅]]より[[伊勢市コミュニティバス|おかげバス]]（[[コミュニティバス]]）「鹿海・朝熊ルート」乗車、「東鹿海」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約3分（約270m）または「西鹿海」バス停下車、徒歩約3分（約320m）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 ;自家用車 * [[国道23号]]「鹿海町」[[交差点]]より東進、約2分（約820m）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 * [[伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン]][[朝熊インターチェンジ|朝熊IC]]より約4分（約1.5km）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3
== 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]]
== 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area09/area09_13.jpg 加努弥神社] }} {{Commonscat|Kanumi-jinja}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0301.html 加努弥神社（かぬみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かぬみしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[加努弥神社]] to [[Kanumi Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 加努弥神社 |画像= [[File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg|270px|加努弥神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]鹿海町字大野間1100-2<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php 『式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館｜125社5』]（式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館、2015年5月3日閲覧）</ref> |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 17.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 51.6 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 稲依比女命 |社格= [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''加努弥神社'''（かぬみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|末社]]。内宮の末社16社のうち、第9位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]鹿海町（かのみちょう、かのめちょう）、田んぼの中に鎮座する<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：108ページ</ref>。鹿海の[[集落]]からは少し離れている<ref name="ic"/>。かつては[[五十鈴川]]が社地のすぐ近くを流れており、船着場もあったとされる<ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：60ページ</ref>。
[[神体]]は[[石]]で、[[石畳]]の上に祀られている<ref name="ic"/>。[[社殿]]はない<ref name="gk"/>。
[[祭神]]は稲依比女命（いなよりひめのみこと）<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。[[年神|大歳神]]の子で、五穀の[[守護神]]である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。
社地の[[面積]]は1畝7歩（≒122[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）ある<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ</ref>。神宮125社のうち、内宮・[[豊受大神宮|外宮]]両宮域外に鎮座する神社の中で最も社地の狭い神社である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=コース08 朝熊道ルート 〜朝熊山麓と五十鈴川沿いの神社を訪ねる〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 創建不詳。伊勢神宮末社の定義より『皇大神宮儀式帳』成立、すなわち[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在した<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ</ref>ことになる。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]の創設により、内宮末社でありながら、[[度会県]]管轄の村社に列し、伊勢神宮と度会県から二重に所管されることとなった<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：271ページ</ref>。こうした二重管轄はほかの神宮摂末社でも発生したため、神宮司庁は[[教部省]]に諮ったところ、11の末社が神宮の管轄から外れた一方、加努弥神社と外宮摂社の[[御食神社 (伊勢市)|御食神社]]はそのまま二重管轄とすることが[[明治5年]]4月（グレゴリオ暦：1872年5月）に申し渡された<ref name="st1"/>。
加努弥神社では[[1881年]]（明治14年）12月に[[氏子]]が内宮末社と村社の分離を求めた<ref name="st6">櫻井（1991）：276ページ</ref>。鹿海の住民は、明治4年に本来は鎮座地も祭神も異なっていた内宮末社と村社が混同され、事実上神宮所管となり、村社として住民が管理できなくなってしまったと訴えた<ref name="st6"/>。更に、[[明治3年]]（[[1870年]]）に村社「八王子社」が「加努弥神社」に改称され、祭神も加努弥神社の祭神に改められたという書類と「加努弥神社」の旧社地が「八王子社」の社地と異なることを示す書類を証拠として提出した<ref name="st7">櫻井（1991）：277ページ</ref>。神宮・[[三重県庁]]・[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]の三者で協議を重ねた結果、[[1886年]]（明治19年）2月に内宮末社と村社を分離することで決着した<ref name="st7"/>。同じく二重管轄とされた御食神社には住民の分離運動が行われたという記録はなく、分離問題はなかったと見られている<ref>櫻井（1991）：278ページ</ref>。
== 交通 == 社地は[[農道]]に面している<ref name="ic"/>。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[五十鈴ケ丘駅]]より徒歩約16分（約1.3km）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[五十鈴川駅]]より[[伊勢市コミュニティバス|おかげバス]]（[[コミュニティバス]]）「鹿海・朝熊ルート」乗車、「東鹿海」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約3分（約270m）または「西鹿海」バス停下車、徒歩約3分（約320m）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 ;自家用車 * [[国道23号]]「鹿海町」[[交差点]]より東進、約2分（約820m）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。 * [[伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン]][[朝熊インターチェンジ|朝熊IC]]より約4分（約1.5km）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3
== 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|qq=|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|qq=|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|qq=|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3
== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area09/area09_13.jpg 加努弥神社] }} {{Commonscat|Kanumi-jinja}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0301.html 加努弥神社（かぬみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
