'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|qq=|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|qq=|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|qq=|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|qq=|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|qq=|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).

; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|qq=|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|qq=|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.

The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.

In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. == Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.

; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3

'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kanumi-jinja]][[ja:加努弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225472]] Wikidata name: Kanumi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kanumi-jinja02.jpg | location = Ōnoma 1100-2, [[Shikami Town]], [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan<ref>[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan｜125 Shrines 5"] (Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengu-kan, accessed May 3, 2015)</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|34.488278|N|136.747667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Inayorihime-no-Mikoto (*稲依比女命*) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = None | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }}
'''{{ill|Kanumi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanumi Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kanumi Jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (*massha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}

It Ranks ninth among the sixteen subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14.</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Kanomi Shrine|ja|加努弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanomi Shrine|1=Kanumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225472}} is located in Kanomi Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, amidst rice fields<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.108</ref>. It is situated slightly away from the main settlement of Kanomi<ref name="ic"/>. Historically, the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] used to flow close to the shrine grounds, and it is said that there was a boat dock nearby<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>.
The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a stone, enshrined on a stone pavement<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building (shaden)<ref name="gk"/>.
The enshrined deity is '''Inayorihime no Mikoto''' (稲依比女命)<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, a daughter of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}, revered as the guardian deity of grains<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 122 m²<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14</ref>, making it the smallest shrine among the 125 shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine located outside the precincts of the Inner and {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|外宮|lt=Gekū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |title=Course 08 Asama Route — Visiting Shrines Along the Asama Mountains and Isuzu River |publisher=Ise City Tourism Association |accessdate=2013-11-16|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1116-2228-54/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course08.html |archivedate=2013-11-16}}</ref>.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown, but it is confirmed to have existed before 804 (Enryaku 23), based on the compilation of the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō''<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.22</ref>. In 1871 (Meiji 4), with the establishment of the modern shrine Ranking system, despite being an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, it was classified as a village shrine under the jurisdiction of Watarai Prefecture, thus falling under dual jurisdiction from both the Ise Grand Shrine and Watarai Prefecture<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.271</ref>.
This dual jurisdiction also occurred with other auxiliary shrines, prompting the Jingū Office to consult with the Ministry of Education. As a result, 11 auxiliary shrines were removed from the Jingū's jurisdiction, but Kanomi Shrine and {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Mike Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mike Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine, remained under dual jurisdiction as of April 1872 (Meiji 5)<ref name="st1"/>.
In December 1881 (Meiji 14), parishioners of Kanomi Shrine petitioned for the separation of the Inner Shrine's auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st6">Sakurai (1991): p.276</ref>. The residents of Kanomi claimed that the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine, originally having different locations and deities, had been conflated since 1871, effectively placing them under the control of the Jingū, restricting local management<ref name="st6"/>. They presented documents showing that the village shrine "Hachioji Shrine" had been renamed "Kanomi Shrine" in 1870 (Meiji 3), with its deity changed to match that of Kanomi Shrine<ref name="st7">Sakurai (1991): p.277</ref>. After discussions among the Jingū, Mie Prefectural Office, and Ministry of Home Affairs, it was decided in February 1886 (Meiji 19) to separate the auxiliary shrine and the village shrine<ref name="st7"/>.
No separation movement was recorded for Mike Shrine, suggesting there were no issues regarding its dual jurisdiction<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.278</ref>.
== Access == The shrine is located along a farm road<ref name="ic"/>. There are no parking facilities nearby.
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 16-minute walk (about 1.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] operated by JR Central. * From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}}, take the "Kanomi-Asama Route" {{ill|community bus|ja|コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/community bus|1=community bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31180853}} (Okage Bus), get off at "Higashi Kanomi" bus stop, and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 270 m), or get off at "Nishi Kanomi" bus stop and walk about 3 minutes (approx. 320 m).
; By Car * About 2 minutes (approx. 820 m) heading east from the "Kanomi Town" intersection on National Route 23. * Approximately 4 minutes (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Interchange|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Interchange|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}}
